bullPoints: Strong global presence and brand equity in IT services and digital transformation.
Robust margins and healthy cash flows, with consistent dividend payouts.
Accelerating growth in cloud services (Infosys Cobalt) and generative AI capabilities (Infosys Topaz).
Large contract pipelines and strong relationships with Fortune 500 companies.
High return on equity (ROE) and return on capital employed (ROCE) compared to industry averages.
bearPoints: High attrition rates in the IT sector continue to pressure talent acquisition and retention costs.
Global macroeconomic uncertainties leading to tighter client budgets and slower decision-making.
Dependence on US and European markets makes it vulnerable to geographic economic slowdowns.
Intense competition from global peers like Accenture, TCS, and Wipro pressuring pricing power.
riskFactors: Currency fluctuations (INR vs. USD/EUR) can significantly impact profitability margins.
Rapid technological changes require continuous upskilling and capital expenditure in AI/Cloud.
H-1B and other visa regulation changes in the US could impact onsite delivery costs.
